raw_text:
Open a second remediation round. Do not stamp Phase 17/18 closed yet.

You’ve solved the structural harness problems (big win), but the remaining 63 failures include real contract mismatches and migration/idempotency defects that violate the same “truth” guarantees Phase 17/18 claim. Closing now would be a paper close.

Why this must be another remediation round

Audit migration enum already exists = migration non-idempotency → unacceptable for “fleet migrate” and DR drill.

Type/column drift (customer_id, missing columns) = your tests no longer represent the real schema or your schema is missing what your code expects; either way it’s unresolved truth.

/api/version 401 = ops readiness diagnostics broken by auth policy (or tests wrong). This is exactly Phase 18 surface area.

Envelope/oracle failures = actual invariants failing, not just infra.

So: bd-19e → bd-19k (or bd-19e → bd-19h if you want it smaller) and clear the remaining categories.

Recommended bead structure + priority (tight, single-concern)
P0

bd-19e — Audit migration idempotency fix

Fix enum creation (mutation_class) safely (Postgres-safe “create-if-not-exists” pattern or migration refactor).

Invariant: migrations are re-runnable across environments/tenants.

Blocks: a bunch of audit-related failures.

bd-19f — Env var normalization for audit DB

Pick one canonical name (I’d standardize on AUDIT_DATABASE_URL and allow legacy alias mapping in test harness).

Invariant: tests do not depend on inconsistent env var naming.

bd-19g — /api/version auth policy alignment

Either exempt /api/version (and /api/ready) from auth, or make tests supply a token.

Given ops needs, I recommend exempt version/ready endpoints from auth.

Invariant: diagnostics endpoints are always callable by ops tooling.

bd-19h — Fix genuine envelope/oracle contract failures

Treat these as real regressions until proven otherwise.

Invariant: envelope validation + UNKNOWN protocol + oracle invariants must pass.

P1

bd-19i — AR customer_id schema/test alignment

Decide: is customer_id supposed to be TEXT/UUID or INT? Then align schema + code + tests.

Invariant: IDs are typed consistently across boundary.

bd-19j — Subscriptions customer_id drift resolution

Same approach: either add the column (with migration + usage) or remove test assumptions.

bd-19k — GL accounts insert fix in tests

Update tests to generate UUIDs (or add DB default). Prefer tests generate UUID unless the model should default.

Assignment strategy (3 agents)

CopperRiver: bd-19e (migration idempotency) — highest leverage

PurpleCliff: bd-19i + bd-19j (schema/test drift triage) OR start with bd-19k quick win

MaroonHarbor: bd-19g then bd-19h (auth + contract/invariant)

Closure rule (what “done” means)

After bd-19e..k, run:

cargo test -p e2e-tests --no-fail-fast

Closure stamp only if: failures are 0 (ignored ok only if explicitly justified like notifications stateless). If you end with a handful of intentional ignores, list them with why.

If you want, I’ll output the exact JSON beads for bd-19e..bd-19k in your standard format so you can drop them straight into your system.
